sections.markdown:
# Worked Example: A COOL-MATH Package

Let's build a small package that exports two functions, then "import" it from another package.

```lisp
;;; cool-math.lisp
(defpackage #:cool-math
  (:use #:cl)
  (:export #:square #:cube))

(in-package #:cool-math)

(defun square (x) (* x x))
(defun cube   (x) (* x x x))
```

Now from a separate file or REPL:

```lisp
(defpackage #:my-app
  (:use #:cl)
  (:import-from #:cool-math #:square))

(in-package #:my-app)

(square 5)               ; => 25, used unqualified because we IMPORT-FROM'd it
(cool-math:cube 5)       ; => 125, used qualified because we did NOT import it
```

Three ways to reach the exports:

1. `cool-math:square` — fully qualified, anywhere.
2. `(:import-from #:cool-math #:square)` — bring just `square` into your package; use unqualified.
3. `(:use #:cool-math)` — bring **every** export into your package. Convenient, but increases collision risk; many shops prefer `:import-from`.

Guarantees the exporter signs up for: every symbol in the `:export` list is interned in the package and made external. Anything else is internal — accessible only via `cool-math::name` (double colon).

sections.code: (progn (defpackage #:cool-math (:use #:cl) (:export #:square #:cube)) (in-package #:cool-math) (defun square (x) (* x x)) (defun cube (x) (* x x x)) (in-package #:cl-user) (cool-math:square 6))
sections.explain: Define the package + functions, then call SQUARE qualified from CL-USER.

sections.code: (progn (defpackage #:cool-math (:use #:cl) (:export #:square #:cube)) (in-package #:cool-math) (defun square (x) (* x x)) (defun cube (x) (* x x x)) (in-package #:cl-user) (cool-math:cube 4))

sections.explain: SECRET is internal. The double colon (::) lets us call it anyway — useful for debugging, smelly in production code.

sections.prompt: Define a package COOL-MATH2 that uses CL and exports HALF and DOUBLE. Define HALF (X) returning X / 2 (use /, not floor) and DOUBLE (X) returning 2 * X. Both go in the COOL-MATH2 package. Tests will call them as COOL-MATH2:HALF and COOL-MATH2:DOUBLE.
sections.hint: (defun half (x) (/ x 2)) inside the COOL-MATH2 package; (defun double (x) (* 2 x)) likewise.
